The Piper genus consists of various species of flowering plants, commonly known as pepper plants, that belong to the family Piperaceae. The health benefits of Piper plants include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain, fever, and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Piper plants include alkaloids, such as piperine, piperlongumine, and chavicine, which have been found to have analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Piper plants include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to alleviate digestive issues, respiratory problems, and pain.
